findings.title: Per-case test loop omits three no-write/leak flags that the contract validator does assert
findings.evidence: The `for (const profile of matrix.case_results)` loop in the CP00-130 test asserts most boundary flags but omits `executes_analytics_query`, `executes_export_download`, and `unauthorized_count_exposed`. These three are still enforced for every case in scripts/validate-rp02-permission-kernel-contract.mjs, so coverage exists at the pack level.
findings.recommendation: Optionally add the three assertions to the test loop for symmetry with the validator. Non-blocking — the .mjs validator already enforces them.

parsed_review.rationale: The pack closes exactly the planned 10 units RP02.P08.M05.S20-RP02.P08.M06.S07: three permission/audit terminal rows (next-gate readiness, documentation update, operator summary) and seven synthetic fixture opening evidence rows (Hermes command matrix, evidence field list, changed-file receipt, command result receipt, fixture summary receipt, blocked-claim receipt, permission summary receipt). first_unit_id=RP02.P08.M05.S20, last_unit_id=RP02.P08.M06.S07, micro-phase split 3/7, deliverable split implementation=3/hermes_evidence=7 — all enforced by validatePermissionKernelCp130Coverage and the validator. CP129 inheritance is wired via imports and runtime checks (inherited_hermes_evidence_workflow_result_count===40, inherited_closeout_handoff_no_commit===true, inherited_permission_summary_reference_only===true). Synthetic-only/metadata-only is preserved (synthetic_only/no_real_data true, product_state_effect 'none', evaluator_invoked false, decision null). Terminal rows stay reference-only (documentation_published, commits_pack, marks_production_ready all false; operator_summary hidden_fields_rendered/contains_real_data/persisted false). Synthetic fixture rows stay preview/reference-only (reference_only true, fixture_persisted false), raw disclosure is blocked (changed_files_disclose_raw_diff and command_result_discloses_raw_output false), and blocked-claim/permission-summary receipts are non-persistent and reference-only (blocked_claim_preview_only true, blocked_claim_persisted false, permission_summary_reference_only true, permission_policy_mutated false). The full no_write_attestation (19 flags) is false in both the source contract and contract JSON, with no runtime UI/API route, policy mutation, audit/product/database write, idempotency/lock persistence, retry/rollback/compensation, export/share, AI/analytics, LDIP, or HRX-split logic introduced (HRX kept embedded). Handoff is fixed to CP00-131 / RP02.P08.M06.S08. The new module is pure frozen-data/functions with no I/O; the only behavioral risk (CP129 case_ids consumed by the inheritance calls) is outside this diff but is gated by the coverage validator and the already-merged CP129. No P0/P1/P2 issues; the single P3 is a cosmetic test-symmetry nit fully compensated by the validator.
